entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: Zea mays L., the primary crop studied for genetic diversity in nontemperate areas

entities.name: CIMMYT
entities.description: International Maize and Wheat Improvement Center, the source of improved breeding material

entities.name: LANDRACES
entities.description: Traditional maize varieties that may contain untapped allelic variation

entities.name: OPEN POLLINATED VARIETIES
entities.description: Modern maize varieties adapted to nontemperate growing areas

entities.name: INBRED LINES
entities.description: Pure maize lines adapted to nontemperate growing areas

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The level of variation in the genetic makeup of maize germplasm

entities.name: ALLELIC VARIATION
entities.description: The presence of different alleles at a locus, useful for future breeding progress

entities.name: SIMPLE SEQUENCE REPEAT MARKERS
entities.description: Molecular markers used to characterize maize individuals and assess diversity

entities.name: MEXICO
entities.description: The country from which maize landraces were sourced for the study

entities.name: NONTEMPERATE GROWING AREAS
entities.description: Environmental regions to which the maize germplasm is adapted

entities.name: HYBRID BREEDING
entities.description: The process of crossing different lines to create hybrids, influenced by genetic resources

entities.name: POPULATION STRUCTURE
entities.description: The distribution of genetic variation within and between germplasm groups
